title: All O`1` Data Structure
problem_statement: Design a data structure supporting increment, decrement, getMaxKey, and getMinKey operations all in O(1) time.

examples.input: Operations include inc("a"), inc("b"), inc("b"), getMaxKey(), getMinKey()
examples.explanation: b has highest count, a has lowest count.

examples.input: After decrementing and removing keys, getMaxKey() and getMinKey() return empty strings.

constraints: Keys are non-empty strings of lowercase letters.
intuition: Use a doubly linked list of count buckets and a hash map from keys to nodes so counts can be updated and extremes can be read in O(1).

approaches.name: Bucket List with HashMaps
approaches.description: Maintain a linked list of frequency nodes and a map from keys to their bucket to support constant-time updates.

interview_qa.q: Why use a linked list of buckets?
interview_qa.a: It lets you move keys between counts and still access min/max counts in constant time.

interview_qa.q: What does the key-to-node map store?
interview_qa.a: It stores current bucket references for each key to update counts quickly.
